task_completion.description: Create a circle in the default sketch plane with a 5 mm radius, centered at (25, 0).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D circle is visible in the sketch plane. There should be dimension constraints showing a radius of 5 (or diameter of 10) and a distance of 25 from the origin.

task_completion.description: Extrude the sketched circle into a 10 mm tall cylindrical post.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D circle is extruded into a 3D cylinder. The property panel or dimension constraint shows an extrusion depth/height of 10 mm.

task_completion.description: Apply the step rotating group feature to the extruded post.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace property browser or group list shows a new 'Step rotating' group has been created and is active.

task_completion.description: Configure the step rotating group for 60 degrees per step and a total of six posts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view shows a circular array of exactly 6 identical cylindrical posts. The property browser for the step rotating group shows '5' additional copies (or 6 total) and an angle of '60' degrees.

task_completion.description: Save the completed model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or the SolveSpace application title bar displays the filename 'circular_post_array.slvs' and indicates the path '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace graphical user interface to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application window with menus, toolbars, and property browsers being actively used, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually generated 3D geometry rather than drawing a 2D representation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The workspace shows a 3D perspective view of the cylinders with proper shading, wireframes, and coordinate axes typical of SolveSpace's 3D environment.
Screenshots show the progression from an initial 2D sketch to a single 3D extrusion, and finally to the completed circular array, proving the file was not simply copied from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/circular_post_array.slvs. The step rotating group must be configured with exactly 5 additional copies (making 6 total posts) and a rotation angle of 60.0 degrees per step (acceptable tolerance +/- 2.0 degrees).
